context: Executor run against https://www.kmart.com.au/product/junk-journal-sticker-pad-43671588/ variant SKU 43552146. Transport: node-tls-client (Chrome fingerprint). Residential proxy AU. Hyper SDK solves www _abck successfully. First failure is api-host sensor.

steps_summary.note: fetched Akamai sensor JS from www
Hyper sensor accepted on www, _abck ind changes

POST to api.kmart.com.au sensor endpoint. apiHostSensor payload=3; bodySuccess=unknown; setCookies=[bm_sv]; wwwAbckBefore=838b ind=0; wwwAbckAfter=838b ind=0; body=<html><title>503 Service Temporarily Unavailable</title>...
loop reports solved rounds=1 despite 503 (false positive in adapter)
GET https://api.kmart.com.au/gateway/graphql (activeCart query). 393b Access Denied HTML from errors.edgesuite.net
id=null v=? postcode=4160. Same edgesuite Access Denied HTML

key_observations: api-host _abck is never seeded — only www _abck exists in jar.
The api_sensor POST returns 503 (not 201). Adapter incorrectly flags it 'solved'.
cart_get / cart_create receive edgesuite 'Access Denied' — Akamai edge, not origin — meaning no valid abck cookie is being sent to api.kmart.com.au.
Question for Harvey: does a real browser hit a distinct sensor endpoint on api.kmart.com.au, or does it reuse the www abck cross-domain? What path/headers does it use?
